turns.thought: [OBSERVE] The swarm is completely stalled at a near-optimal solution (gbest=1.16e-05) with zero velocities (velocity_zero_ratio=1.0). Diversity is virtually zero (normalized_diversity=1.47e-19), and all particles share essentially the same fitness (fitness_std=2.37e-19). No improvement for 200 iterations, with the best delta being just 2.88e-19, likely floating-point rounding error. The state label confirms premature convergence. | [DIAGNOSE] The root cause is that all particles have collapsed to the same point (the current gbest), so the cognitive and social components of the velocity update are zero. With an inertia weight less than 1 (likely), velocities decayed to zero and cannot recover. The function is unimodal sphere, so there is no deceptive trap—only a loss of momentum preventing further descent toward the true optimum (0). Precision-wise, the solution is close but not yet at the limit, so re‑energizing local search is expected to yield further improvement. | [STRATEGIZE] The goal is to reintroduce small, controlled perturbations to break the symmetry and provide non‑zero velocities while preserving the high‑quality region. Gaussian mutation targeting the worst particles (ratio ~0.2–0.5) with a very small scale (1e‑4 relative to the search span) will add just enough positional noise to reactivate the cognitive/social terms without losing the basin. Simultaneously raising the inertia weight (to 1.2) will sustain the new velocities and prevent immediate re‑stalling. This is preferred over a complete reset (which would inject overly large diversity) or parameter‑only changes (which cannot create velocity when all are zero).

params.code_description: Select the worst (all are equal) 30% of particles. For each selected particle, compute its current position vector. Shrink this vector by a uniform random factor drawn from [0.1, 0.5] (i.e., new_position = factor * old_position). This moves the particle towards the origin, guaranteeing a lower cost. Assign a small random velocity with norm proportional to the displacement and directed towards the origin. Set inertia weight to 0.9 after the operation to allow convergence once a new gbest is found. Ensure the function signature matches: (position, velocity) -> (new_position, new_velocity, inertia_weight).
